task: Implement a JavaScript function `compareVersions(a, b)` that compares two semantic-version strings and returns -1 if a < b, 0 if a == b, and 1 if a > b. Each version is dot-separated numeric release identifiers (e.g. '1.2.0'), optionally followed by a hyphen and a dot-separated pre-release tag (e.g. '1.2.0-alpha.1'). Rules, applied strictly in order: (1) Compare the numeric release fields left to right, comparing each NUMERICALLY (so 2 < 10); a missing field is treated as 0. (2) When the release fields are equal, a version that has NO pre-release tag has HIGHER precedence than one that does. (3) When both have a pre-release tag, compare its identifiers left to right: a purely numeric identifier is compared numerically and always ranks LOWER than a non-numeric identifier; two non-numeric identifiers compare lexically by ASCII order. (4) If all compared identifiers are equal, the version with FEWER pre-release identifiers ranks lower. These are adversarial cases: numeric-vs-lexical ordering of identifiers and the release-outranks-prerelease rule are where naive string comparison fails.
visibleSpec: compareVersions(a, b) -> -1 | 0 | 1. Inputs are valid SemVer-like strings 'X.Y.Z' or 'X.Y.Z-pre.release.ids'. Release fields compare numerically; release without a pre-release tag outranks the same release with one; pre-release identifiers compare per SemVer precedence (numeric < alphanumeric, numeric compared numerically, alphanumeric compared by ASCII, fewer identifiers ranks lower when otherwise equal).
scope: Single pure function. No mutation of inputs, no extra parameters, no helper globals, no I/O, no external libraries.
